confidence_meta.reason: H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances release due today (Jul 16) and still pending as of this scrape

structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: Consumer credit / financials (ALLY, COF) favored over Asia-exposed semis; broad duration neutral pending H.4.1
structured_conclusion.next_catalyst: H.4.1 Fed balance sheet release — due today, still pending
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: 1/3 — USDKRW below 1,530 PASS; EWY below SMA20 FAIL; KOSPI below 8,200 FAIL

bias: Structural liquidity data hasn't refreshed since Jul 8 and still points to easing (TGA drawdown, rising reserves), but the reverse repo buffer has drained to near zero, removing the shock absorber that cushioned liquidity swings for three years. Today's move is dominated by an idiosyncratic Korea/semiconductor selloff — SK Hynix and Samsung down double digits/high single digits into next week's earnings — rather than a broad macro liquidity or credit event; US credit and bond vol (MOVE) actually improved on the day even as nominal yields backed up.

counterargs.thesis: Liquidity backdrop still easing at the margin, but today's selloff is a Korea/semis-specific risk event, not a regime shift
supporting: MOVE fell 8.74% to 68.48 — bond-market vol easing sharply
HY OAS flat, ALLY (+2.18%) and COF (+1.75%) rallied — credit calm
USDKRW comfortably below the 1,530 gate threshold
TGA/reserves data (last confirmed Jul 8) still shows drawdown/build trend intact
against: SK Hynix -11.53%, Samsung -8.77%, KOSPI -6.37% — sharp Korea tech rout ahead of Jul 20 SK Hynix earnings
US yields rose across the curve (10Y +4.3bp, 2Y +3.4bp) even as bond vol fell — an unusual divergence
RRP drained to $0.151B, effectively exhausting the Fed's liquidity buffer
Oil pushed above $80 (+1.11% to $80.48), extending its weekly uptrend and threatening the disinflation narrative
Korea gate still closed 2 of 3 conditions; EWY fell further below its 20-day SMA
counterargs.read: This looks like an idiosyncratic Korea semiconductor risk-off event — SK Hynix and Samsung selling off hard into next week's earnings, dragging KOSPI and EWY — layered on top of a US liquidity backdrop that is still nominally easing but running out of buffer (RRP near zero) and showing a real-rate warning sign (2Y breakeven inflation collapsed 32bp today while nominal 2Y yields rose, consistent with the real-rate-tightening pain trade flagged in earlier runs). Credit and broad bond vol stayed calm, arguing against a systemic liquidity event, but the Asia tech move and the still-pending H.4.1 print are the two things to watch before drawing a firmer conclusion.

trade_table.rationale: Nominal yields rose across the curve while the 2Y breakeven collapsed 32bp — a real-rate-tightening signature, though 5Y/10Y breakevens stayed flat.
ALLY and COF both rallying, HY OAS flat, MOVE down sharply — credit conditions calm.
SK Hynix -11.5%, Samsung -8.8%, KOSPI -6.4%, DRAM -6.3%, SOXX -2.2% — sharp risk-off into SK Hynix's ~Jul 20 earnings.
Gate closed 1/3; EWY well below SMA20 and falling further today.
Oil above $80 for the first time this stretch; 2Y breakeven collapse muddies the read — not a clean reacceleration signal yet.

catalysts.event: H.4.1 Factors Affecting Reserve Balances release (WALCL/TGA/Reserves)
catalysts.note: Due today; not yet posted as of this scrape — will confirm whether the TGA drawdown/reserve build trend continued
Elevated importance after today's -11.5% pre-earnings selloff; KIS has flagged possible HBM pricing miss risk

plumbing.rrp_delta: drained further, from $0.278B — buffer nearly gone

duration_score.reason: 10Y and 2Y both rose today while the 2Y breakeven (EXPINF2YR) collapsed 32bp — nominal yields firming against falling short-end inflation expectations is the real-rate-tightening signature, even though 5Y/10Y breakevens stayed flat and MOVE fell sharply.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Front-end real yields pushing higher again even as bond-market vol (MOVE) eases — a real-rate story, not a broad risk-off.
james_framework.pain_trade: Real-rate tightening. Not CPI panic — 2Y breakeven collapsing while nominal 2Y firms.
james_framework.watch_signal: If nominal 2Y starts falling while breakevens stabilize, the pain trade eases; if nominal 2Y keeps rising with breakevens falling further, real-rate pressure intensifies.

breakevens.breakeven_curve_signal: 5Y/10Y breakevens flat while the 2Y breakeven collapsed and nominal yields rose — a front-end real-rate-tightening signature distinct from the broader disinflation story further out the curve.
